title: hieracium murorum subsp. obversum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. obversum, also known as wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium murorum plant, characterized by its yellow flowers and sprawling habit. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful for treating various health conditions, including skin irritations and infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Hieracium murorum subsp. obversum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various applications in traditional medicine and modern herbalism.
